children.label: A
children.text: information demonstrating to the Secretary’s satisfaction that the State has in effect the statewide system required by ; andsection 1433 of this title

children.text: a description of services to be provided to infants and toddlers with disabilities and their families through the system;
if the State provides services to at-risk infants and toddlers through the statewide system, a description of such services;
a description of the uses for which funds will be expended in accordance with this subchapter;

children.label: A
children.text: is involved in a substantiated case of child abuse or neglect; or

children.label: B
children.text: is identified as affected by illegal substance abuse, or withdrawal symptoms resulting from prenatal drug exposure;
a description of the procedure used to ensure that resources are made available under this subchapter for all geographic areas within the State;

a description of State policies and procedures that ensure that, prior to the adoption by the State of any other policy or procedure necessary to meet the requirements of this subchapter, there are public hearings, adequate notice of the hearings, and an opportunity for comment available to the general public, including individuals with disabilities and parents of infants and toddlers with disabilities;

children.label: i
children.text: the families of such toddlers and children will be included in the transition plans required by subparagraph (C); and

children.label: I
children.text: notify the local educational agency for the area in which such a child resides that the child will shortly reach the age of eligibility for preschool services under subchapter II, as determined in accordance with State law;

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a child who may be eligible for such preschool services, with the approval of the family of the child, convene a conference among the lead agency, the family, and the local educational agency not less than 90 days (and at the discretion of all such parties, not more than 9 months) before the child is eligible for the preschool services, to discuss any such services that the child may receive; and

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of a child who may not be eligible for such preschool services, with the approval of the family, make reasonable efforts to convene a conference among the lead agency, the family, and providers of other appropriate services for children who are not eligible for preschool services under subchapter II, to discuss the appropriate services that the child may receive;

children.label: B
children.text: to review the child’s program options for the period from the child’s third birthday through the remainder of the school year; and

children.label: C
children.text: to establish a transition plan, including, as appropriate, steps to exit from the program;

a description of State efforts to promote collaboration among Early Head Start programs under , early education and child care programs, and services under this subchapter; andsection 9840a of title 42
such other information and assurances as the Secretary may reasonably require.
shall provide satisfactory assurance that Federal funds made available under to the State will be expended in accordance with this subchapter;section 1443 of this title
shall contain an assurance that the State will comply with the requirements of ;section 1440 of this title

shall provide satisfactory assurance that the control of funds provided under , and title to property derived from those funds, will be in a public agency for the uses and purposes provided in this subchapter and that a public agency will administer such funds and property;section 1443 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: making such reports in such form and containing such information as the Secretary may require to carry out the Secretary’s functions under this subchapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: keeping such reports and affording such access to the reports as the Secretary may find necessary to ensure the correctness and verification of those reports and proper disbursement of Federal funds under this subchapter;

children.label: A
children.text: will not be commingled with State funds; and

children.label: B
children.text: will be used so as to supplement the level of State and local funds expended for infants and toddlers with disabilities and their families and in no case to supplant those State and local funds;

shall provide satisfactory assurance that such fiscal control and fund accounting procedures will be adopted as may be necessary to ensure proper disbursement of, and accounting for, Federal funds paid under to the State;section 1443 of this title

shall provide satisfactory assurance that policies and procedures have been adopted to ensure meaningful involvement of underserved groups, including minority, low-income, homeless, and rural families and children with disabilities who are wards of the State, in the planning and implementation of all the requirements of this subchapter; and
shall contain such other information and assurances as the Secretary may reasonably require by regulation.

children.heading: Standard for disapproval of application
children.label: c Standard for disapproval of application
children.text: The Secretary may not disapprove such an application unless the Secretary determines, after notice and opportunity for a hearing, that the application fails to comply with the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Subsequent State application
children.label: d Subsequent State application
children.text: If a State has on file with the Secretary a policy, procedure, or assurance that demonstrates that the State meets a requirement of this section, including any policy or procedure filed under this subchapter (as in effect before ), the Secretary shall consider the State to have met the requirement for purposes of receiving a grant under this subchapter.December 3, 2004

children.heading: Modification of application
children.label: e Modification of application
children.text: An application submitted by a State in accordance with this section shall remain in effect until the State submits to the Secretary such modifications as the State determines necessary. This section shall apply to a modification of an application to the same extent and in the same manner as this section applies to the original application.

children.heading: Modifications required by the Secretary
children.label: f Modifications required by the Secretary

children.text: an amendment is made to this chapter, or a Federal regulation issued under this chapter;
a new interpretation of this chapter is made by a Federal court or the State’s highest court; or
an official finding of noncompliance with Federal law or regulations is made with respect to the State.
